mnemonics: Think RGB: Red, Green, Blue noise types. Each channel gets its own 'color' of noise, just like the channels themselves.
keyPoints: Noise can be independent per color channel (e.g., Gaussian noise in Red, Green, Blue)
Noise can be correlated across channels, affecting color balance
Denoising must be applied carefully to preserve color relationships and avoid introducing color artifacts
commonMistakes: Assuming noise affects all color channels equally and applying the same denoising filter uniformly
Aggressively filtering noise which can result in loss of color saturation or a washed-out appearance
